title: Model Provider Plugins
summary: Guide for building model provider plugins using ProviderProfile, covering discovery order, auto-wire integrations (auth, CLI, model picker, doctor, setup wizard, runtime resolution), ProviderProfile field reference, overridable hooks (prepare_messages, build_extra_body, fetch_models), api_mode selection, auth types, and user overrides.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify a new model provider plugin loaded correctly
Smoke test a newly registered provider

code_examples.caption: Minimal API-key provider plugin
code_examples.code:
from providers import register_provider
from providers.base import ProviderProfile

acme = ProviderProfile(
    name="acme-inference",
    aliases=("acme",),
    env_vars=("ACME_API_KEY", "ACME_BASE_URL"),
    base_url="https://api.acme.example.com/v1",
    auth_type="api_key",
    default_aux_model="acme-small-fast",
    fallback_models=("acme-large-v3", "acme-small-fast",),
)
register_provider(acme)

code_examples.caption: Subclass for provider-specific quirks

gotchas: Provider discovery is LAZY — triggered by first get_provider_profile() or list_providers() call
User plugins at $HERMES_HOME/plugins/model-providers/<name>/ override bundled plugins of same name (last-writer-wins)
plugin.yaml is optional but recommended — without it the general loader uses source-text heuristics
auth_type='api_key' is required for doctor checks, --provider flag, and setup wizard automation
The general PluginManager sees model-provider plugins but does not import them — providers/__init__.py owns their lifecycle
full_text:
# Building a Model Provider Plugin

Model provider plugins declare an inference backend — an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, an Anthropic Messages server, a Codex-style Responses API, or a Bedrock-native surface — that Hermes can route `AIAgent` calls through. Every built-in provider (OpenRouter, Anthropic, GMI, DeepSeek, Nvidia, etc.) ships as one of these plugins.

## How discovery works

`providers/__init__.py._discover_providers()` runs lazily the first time any code calls `get_provider_profile()` or `list_providers()`. Discovery order:

1. **Bundled plugins** — `<repo>/plugins/model-providers/<name>/`
2. **User plugins** — `$HERMES_HOME/plugins/model-providers/<name>/`
3. **Legacy single-file** — `<repo>/providers/<name>.py`

User plugins override bundled plugins of the same name because `register_provider()` is last-writer-wins.

## Directory structure

```
plugins/model-providers/my-provider/
__init__.py       # Calls register_provider(profile) at module-level
plugin.yaml       # kind: model-provider + metadata (optional but recommended)
README.md         # Setup instructions (optional)
```

The only required file is `__init__.py`.

## Minimal example

```python
from providers import register_provider
from providers.base import ProviderProfile

acme = ProviderProfile(
    name="acme-inference",
    aliases=("acme",),
    display_name="Acme Inference",
    description="Acme — OpenAI-compatible direct API",
    signup_url="https://acme.example.com/keys",
    env_vars=("ACME_API_KEY", "ACME_BASE_URL"),
    base_url="https://api.acme.example.com/v1",
    auth_type="api_key",
    default_aux_model="acme-small-fast",
    fallback_models=(
        "acme-large-v3",
        "acme-medium-v3",
        "acme-small-fast",
    ),
)

register_provider(acme)
```

After dropping these two files, the following auto-wire with no other edits: credential resolution, `--provider` CLI flag, `hermes model` picker, `hermes doctor` health check, `hermes setup` wizard, URL reverse-mapping, auxiliary model, runtime resolution, transport.

## ProviderProfile fields

| Field | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | str | Canonical id |
| `aliases` | tuple | Alternative names resolved by `get_provider_profile()` |
| `api_mode` | str | `chat_completions` / `codex_responses` / `anthropic_messages` / `bedrock_converse` |
| `display_name` | str | Human label shown in `hermes model` picker |
| `description` | str | Picker subtitle |
| `signup_url` | str | Shown during first-run setup |
| `env_vars` | tuple | API-key env vars in priority order |
| `base_url` | str | Default inference endpoint |
| `models_url` | str | Explicit catalog URL |
| `auth_type` | str | `api_key` / `oauth_device_code` / `oauth_external` / `copilot` / `aws_sdk` / `external_process` |
| `fallback_models` | tuple | Curated list shown when live catalog fetch fails |
| `default_headers` | dict | Sent on every request |
| `fixed_temperature` | Any | None = use caller's value |
| `default_max_tokens` | int / None | Provider-level max_tokens cap |
| `default_aux_model` | str | Cheap model for auxiliary tasks |

## Overridable hooks

Subclass `ProviderProfile` for non-trivial quirks: `prepare_messages()`, `build_extra_body()`, `build_api_kwargs_extras()`, `fetch_models()`.

## Hook reference examples

| Plugin | Why look |
|---|---|
| `plugins/model-providers/openrouter/` | Aggregator with provider preferences, public model catalog |
| `plugins/model-providers/gemini/` | `thinking_config` translation |
| `plugins/model-providers/kimi-coding/` | `OMIT_TEMPERATURE`, `extra_body.thinking`, top-level `reasoning_effort` |
| `plugins/model-providers/qwen-oauth/` | Message normalization, `cache_control` injection |
| `plugins/model-providers/nous/` | Attribution tags |
| `plugins/model-providers/bedrock/` | `api_mode='bedrock_converse'`, `fetch_models` returns None |

## api_mode selection

Four values are recognized. Hermes picks one based on: user explicit override, OpenCode's per-model dispatch, URL auto-detection, profile `api_mode` as fallback, default `chat_completions`.

## Auth types

| `auth_type` | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `api_key` | Single env var carries a static API key |
| `oauth_device_code` | Device-code OAuth flow |
| `oauth_external` | User signs in elsewhere, tokens land in `auth.json` |
| `copilot` | GitHub Copilot token refresh cycle |
| `aws_sdk` | AWS SDK credential chain |
| `external_process` | Auth handled by a subprocess |

## Discovery timing

Provider discovery is **lazy** — triggered by the first `get_provider_profile()` or `list_providers()` call. Verify your plugin loaded with `hermes doctor`.

## User overrides — replace a built-in without editing the repo

Create `~/.hermes/plugins/model-providers/<name>/__init__.py` with a `register_provider()` call. Next session, the user profile wins because user plugins are discovered after bundled ones.

## Distribute via pip

```toml
[project.entry-points."hermes.plugins"]
acme-inference = "acme_hermes_plugin:register"
```
